text: I find myself standing at the threshold of two worlds. Outside the glass, I see people engaged in focused discussion, perhaps the birth of new ideas or the quiet progress of familiar knowledge. They are observed from a distance, their energy contained and purposeful—a microcosm of society's pursuit of understanding. I feel like an outsider looking in, both curious and contemplative. Something about the sterile orderliness mixed with the tangible anticipation of learned discovery gives me both comfort and a sense of longing to participate.
